title: refactor(release): centralize package location resolution
summary: Clean, well-structured refactor that correctly centralizes repo-relative path derivation in a single abstraction. Tests cover custom layouts, standard layouts, out-of-root rejection, and `containsRepoPath` matching. Two findings: one dead-code guard in `fromAbsolutePath` and one undocumented behavioral change from silent-skip to Effect-defect for out-of-root packages.

findings.title: Dead code: second TypeError guard in `fromAbsolutePath` is unreachable
findings.body: The guard `if (relativePath.length === 0)` with the message `"cannot be the repo root itself"` is unreachable. `normalizeAbsoluteDir` strips trailing slashes from both `root` and `path`, so when `path === root`, both normalize to the same string (e.g. `/repo`). Then `normalizedPath.startsWith(rootPrefix)` = `'/repo'.startsWith('/repo/')` â†’ `false`, so the first `TypeError` fires instead with the misleading message `"is not inside repo root"`. The second guard never executes. Either the normalization should preserve a trailing slash on `rootPrefix` differently, or the error message on the first guard should cover the root-equality case explicitly.

findings.title: Silent skip â†’ Effect defect: behavioral change in `toAffectedPackages` is undocumented
findings.body:
The old `toAffectedPackages` silently skipped packages whose absolute path wasn't inside the repo root (via a `null` filter). The new code calls `PackageLocation.fromAbsolutePath(root, pkg.path)` inside `.map()`, which throws a synchronous `TypeError` for any out-of-root package. Since `toAffectedPackages` is called as a plain function inside `Effect.gen` (not yielded), Effect's generator runner converts the uncaught throw into a defect (`Effect.die`), failing the entire `loadPullRequestDiff` effect rather than skipping the problematic package.

This is arguably the correct behavior (out-of-root packages are a programmer error), but it's an observable breaking change from the prior graceful degradation. A comment on `fromAbsolutePath` or a short rationale note clarifying the fail-fast intent would make the contract explicit.

residual_risks: Branch names or package directory names containing URL-special characters (e.g. `#`, `?`, spaces) are not percent-encoded in `toSourceUrl`. The string interpolation `https://github.com/.../tree/${branch}/${relativePath}` would produce invalid URLs. This existed before the refactor (the old inline template strings had the same issue), so not introduced here â€” but worth tracking as a pre-existing gap now surfaced clearly.

`normalizeRelativeDir` accepts `Fs.Path.RelDir | string`, making the union open to arbitrary strings at call sites without branded-type guarantees. Currently all call sites pass known-good values, but the type signature doesn't enforce it.
